name: Carbon Fiber Reinforced Polymer Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Carbon Fiber Reinforced Polymer. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for CFRP cleaning without matrix damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for optimal carbon absorption and minimal polymer damage

spotSize.description: Focused spot size for precise cleaning resolution

repetitionRate.description: High repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without CFRP damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive heating

power.description: Typical range for pulsed Nd:YAG lasers in CFRP cleaning to ablate contaminants without matrix degradation; starting at 30W for light contamination. Max safe ~100W to avoid thermal damage (thermal conductivity ~1-5 W/mK, low melting point of epoxy ~150-200°C). Source: Journal of Laser Applications (2018) on composite cleaning.

scan_speed.description: Effective range balances cleaning efficiency and heat input; recommended 1500 mm/s for optimal CFRP surface prep. Slower (500 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (3000 mm/s) for light. Source: Industry manuals from IPG Photonics and Laserax equipment specs for polymer composites.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for CFRP to minimize heat-affected zone (HAZ <50 μm); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond for ultra-precise but costlier. 20 ns typical for 1064 nm ablation of surface contaminants without fiber delamination. Source: Laser physics studies in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2020).

repetition_rate.description: Standard for industrial cleaning; 30 kHz recommended for CFRP to achieve fast processing while limiting heat accumulation in low-conductivity matrix. Higher rates increase speed but risk overheating. Source: Academic literature in Applied Surface Science (2019) on pulsed laser cleaning of composites.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for contaminant removal (e.g., oils, paints); CFRP damage threshold ~2-5 J/cm² depending on matrix. Recommended 1.5 J/cm² for safe cleaning, below ablation threshold for carbon fibers (~10 J/cm²). Conservative to account for reflectivity (~30-50% at 1064 nm). Source: Ablation threshold studies in Composites Part B (2017).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for precision vs. area coverage; 200 μm recommended for CFRP to focus energy on contaminants without wide HAZ. Smaller for fine details, larger for broad surfaces. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser cleaning systems for composites.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete CFRP cleaning with minimal damage risk. Multi-pass allows lower fluence per pass. Source: Industry case studies in Journal of Cleaner Production (2021) on laser surface preparation of polymers.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage without excessive overlap heating; 60% recommended for CFRP to achieve even cleaning while controlling thermal load. Typical 30-80% in optimization. Source: Process studies in International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology (2022).
